page: Microsoft Azure HB120-16rs_v2 is an x86_64 architecture server instance belonging to the HBrsv2 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 16 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel processor. The instance is equipped with 456.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 28.5 GB per vCPU.

For local storage, the server includes 1452 GB of local storage capacity and supports Premium Storage. Additionally, the instance is RDMA (Remote Direct Memory Access) capable, facilitating low-latency, high-throughput networking interfaces for clustered environments. It does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses or dedicated GPU hardware.

Given the high memory allocation of 28.5 GB per vCPU, this instance offers high resource density for memory-bound tasks. The combination of dedicated vCPUs, RDMA capabilities, and substantial local storage makes this server configuration suitable for memory-intensive workloads, large-scale database hosting, and clustered high-performance computing nodes that do not require GPU acceleration.
